roadmap.notes: Lands ADR-0054 (CgroupFs port trait + RealCgroupFs host adapter + SimCgroupFs sim adapter + CgroupManager struct refactor + ExecDriver constructor injection + binary composition-root probe gate). Single-cut greenfield migration per project posture; no compatibility shims, no #[deprecated], no cfg(test) swaps. The 8-step sequence below matches ADR-0054 § D5 'Migration plan' verbatim. Every step's scenario_name field references docs/feature/cgroup-fs-port/distill/test-scenarios.md (project ban on .feature files per .claude/rules/testing.md — GIVEN/WHEN/THEN scenarios are specification prose, materialised by the crafter into Rust #[test] / #[tokio::test] / proptest! / trybuild cases). Mutation gate per .claude/rules/testing.md § Per-step vs per-PR scoping: per-step `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-worker --features integration-tests --file <step's-touched-files>` with kill rate ≥ 80%; final per-PR run drops --file. Lima discipline mandatory for every integration-tests-gated invocation per .claude/rules/testing.md § Running tests — Lima VM.

phases.name: CgroupFs port trait introduction + composition migration
phases.description: ADR-0054 single-cut migration: introduce the narrow CgroupFs port trait in overdrive-core, the RealCgroupFs host adapter in overdrive-host (wrapping tokio::fs::*), the SimCgroupFs sim adapter in overdrive-sim (BTreeMap byte store + injection schedule), refactor the eight free functions in overdrive-worker::cgroup_manager into methods on a new CgroupManager struct holding Arc<dyn CgroupFs>, change ExecDriver::new to accept the trait object mandatory-not-defaulted, wire the binary composition root to instantiate-probe-then-thread the Arc<dyn CgroupFs> through the worker subsystem entrypoint, triage the 16 existing inline tests per the DISTILL E1 matrix (5 keep-inline + 7 convert + 2 keep-and-move + 2 keep-tempfile), and add the production-realistic substrate-boundary Tier 3 coverage (Class C scenarios). 8 steps total; every step gates on per-step mutation kill rate ≥ 80%; AC9 (existing Lima integration suite still green) is asserted at step 05 and re-checked at step 08.

steps.name: Introduce CgroupFs trait + ProbeError in overdrive-core; pin trybuild
steps.description: Lands the narrow CgroupFs trait and ProbeError enum per ADR-0054 § D1, placed in overdrive-core/src/traits/cgroup_fs.rs per ADR-0054 § D2. Re-exports CgroupFs and ProbeError from overdrive-core::traits::mod and the crate root. Adds `trybuild = "=1.0.<latest>"` to [workspace.dependencies] (first DISTILL fixture user in this repo per the DISTILL § Pre-requisites note) and `trybuild.workspace = true` under overdrive-worker [dev-dependencies]. Lands the A1 trybuild compile-fail fixture as a stub that's expected to fail until step 01-05 changes the ExecDriver::new arity (the compile-fail .stderr pin reflects the FINAL post-migration error; for steps 01-01 through 01-04 the fixture is wired but not in the trybuild runner's active set, OR the crafter lands it as a #[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")] placeholder per .claude/rules/testing.md § RED scaffolds — the crafter picks the cleanest path that doesn't gate-bypass pre-commit). Lands the B-kind-returns-stable-string contract pin against the trait type-level statement (verifies the docstring promise that operators grep on the string in startup logs).
steps.criteria: CgroupFs trait declared per ADR-0054 § D1 with all five methods (create_dir, write, remove_dir, probe, kind) and their rustdoc preconditions/postconditions/edge-cases/observable-invariants per .claude/rules/development.md § Trait definitions specify behavior. ProbeError enum declared with Substrate { source } and RoundTripMismatch { wrote, read } variants. trybuild pinned exactly in [workspace.dependencies]; trybuild.workspace = true under overdrive-worker [dev-dependencies]. cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace passes; dst-lint passes (no banned APIs in overdrive-core).
steps.scenario_name: A1 (compile-fail trybuild fixture for ExecDriver::new arity — lands wired but expected GREEN at step 01-05); B-kind-returns-stable-string (trait-level contract pin)
acceptance_criteria: crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/cgroup_fs.rs exists with `pub trait CgroupFs: Send + Sync + 'static` and all five async/sync methods per ADR-0054 § D1: `async fn create_dir(&self, path: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()>`, `async fn write(&self, path: &Path, bytes: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<()>`, `async fn remove_dir(&self, path: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()>`, `async fn probe(&self) -> Result<(), ProbeError>`, `fn kind(&self) -> &'static str`

Every trait method's rustdoc pins preconditions, postconditions on Ok, edge cases (idempotency, NotFound, AlreadyExists, DirectoryNotEmpty), observable invariants, and an Errors section enumerating the substrate-specific io::ErrorKind values callers should expect — per .claude/rules/development.md § Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature

ProbeError enum declared in the same file with exactly two variants: `Substrate { #[source] source: std::io::Error }` and `RoundTripMismatch { wrote: Vec<u8>, read: Vec<u8> }`; derives Debug + thiserror::Error; per-variant #[error("...")] strings match ADR-0054 § D1 verbatim so the error chain produces the documented Display form

Trait docstring includes the § Scope block from ADR-0054 § D1 documenting the non-replacement contract (byte side effects only; kernel side effects NOT modelled by SimCgroupFs); operators reading the trait know SimCgroupFs is honest about its scope
Trait docstring includes the § Earned Trust block documenting the composition-root invariant 'wire then probe then use'; surfaces the structural failure mode (health.startup.refused event) explicitly

CgroupFs and ProbeError re-exported from crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/mod.rs (matches the existing Clock/Transport/Entropy/Dataplane re-export pattern) and from the crate root (matches existing port re-exports)

[workspace.dependencies] in root Cargo.toml gains `trybuild = "=1.0.<latest>"` (exact pin per .claude/rules/testing.md § Compile-fail testing → Pin trybuild exactly); rg "^trybuild" Cargo.toml returns exactly one match
crates/overdrive-worker/Cargo.toml gains `trybuild.workspace = true` under [dev-dependencies]

crates/overdrive-worker/tests/compile_fail.rs entrypoint exists wiring the trybuild runner harness; pattern: `#[test] fn compile_fail() { let t = trybuild::TestCases::new(); t.compile_fail("tests/compile_fail/*.rs"); }`

crates/overdrive-worker/tests/compile_fail/exec_driver_missing_fs.rs exists as the A1 stub. Per .claude/rules/testing.md § RED scaffolds, the fixture is wired but NOT yet expected GREEN — crafter chooses either (a) commenting out the fixture's `t.compile_fail(...)` glob and re-enabling at step 01-05, OR (b) landing the fixture with the .stderr matching the current (pre-migration) error shape and updating the .stderr at step 01-05 alongside the constructor change. Document the chosen approach in the fixture's leading comment
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --all-features completes with no errors
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run completes with no errors (compile-only macOS gate per .claude/rules/testing.md)
dst-lint passes — verified by `cargo xtask dst-lint` returning exit 0 (no banned APIs in overdrive-core; the new trait file contains zero tokio/std::fs/Instant references)

Per-step mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/cgroup_fs.rs achieves kill rate ≥ 80% (this step's mutants are minimal — likely only the ProbeError Display strings; vacuous-pass is acceptable per .claude/rules/testing.md § Empty filter intersection)

steps.name: Add RealCgroupFs adapter in overdrive-host wrapping tokio::fs::*
steps.description: Lands the production binding of CgroupFs per ADR-0054 § Real adapter. Wraps tokio::fs::{create_dir_all, write, remove_dir} for the three I/O methods; implements probe() per ADR-0054 § D1 (mkdir tempdir under probe_root → write probe-file with b"probe\n" → tokio::fs::read round-trip → assert byte-equal → remove_file + remove_dir teardown). Exposes a `with_probe_root(PathBuf)` builder for tests (this is NOT a port-trait-injection builder — it's an internal adapter knob; the port-trait-mandatory-not-defaulted rule still applies at ExecDriver::new). Re-exports RealCgroupFs from overdrive-host::lib.rs. No new external dep (tokio, uuid both already in workspace). Lands the C-probe-success and C-probe-with-custom-root Tier 3 scenarios — these GREEN immediately because the host adapter is self-contained; they do not depend on the CgroupManager refactor.
steps.criteria: RealCgroupFs struct exists at crates/overdrive-host/src/cgroup_fs.rs per ADR-0054 § Real adapter; new() / with_probe_root() / CgroupFs impl all match the ADR's reference implementation verbatim; probe() exercises the 4-step round-trip including a tokio::fs::read of the written file (not just write-and-pray); kind() returns the literal string "overdrive_host::RealCgroupFs"; C-probe-success runs green against real /sys/fs/cgroup via Lima sudo; C-probe-with-custom-root runs green against a tempfile::TempDir and asserts /sys/fs/cgroup contents are byte-identical pre/post (proves the override genuinely scopes).
steps.scenario_name: C-probe-success (Tier 3 walking-skeleton happy path against real /sys/fs/cgroup); C-probe-with-custom-root (Tier 3 builder override scoping)
acceptance_criteria: crates/overdrive-host/src/cgroup_fs.rs exists with `pub struct RealCgroupFs { probe_root: PathBuf }` matching ADR-0054 § Real adapter shape
RealCgroupFs::new() returns a value with probe_root = PathBuf::from("/sys/fs/cgroup"); doc comment names the default explicitly

RealCgroupFs::with_probe_root(self, root: PathBuf) -> Self consumes self, sets the field, returns Self (chainable builder); doc comment marks this as TEST-ONLY scoping for the probe — NOT a port-trait injection builder
#[async_trait] impl CgroupFs for RealCgroupFs has create_dir → tokio::fs::create_dir_all, write → tokio::fs::write, remove_dir → tokio::fs::remove_dir (matches ADR-0054 § Real adapter verbatim)

probe() implementation exercises the 4-step round-trip per ADR-0054 § D1 trait docstring → § Production probe (RealCgroupFs): (1) create_dir(&probe_dir) where probe_dir = self.probe_root.join(format!(".overdrive-probe-{}", uuid::Uuid::new_v4())); (2) write(&probe_dir.join("probe-file"), b"probe\n"); (3) tokio::fs::read(&probe_file) — read-back via tokio::fs::read directly because the trait surface has no read method, and the probe MUST demonstrate round-trip (not just write-and-pray); (4) tokio::fs::remove_file then self.remove_dir teardown. On byte-mismatch return ProbeError::RoundTripMismatch { wrote, read }; on any io::Error return ProbeError::Substrate { source }
fn kind(&self) -> &'static str returns exactly the literal "overdrive_host::RealCgroupFs"
RealCgroupFs re-exported from crates/overdrive-host/src/lib.rs (matches existing SystemClock / OsEntropy / TcpTransport re-export pattern)

C-probe-success Tier 3 test exists at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/real_cgroup_fs/probe_success.rs (or similar) — gated behind --features integration-tests; constructs Arc::new(RealCgroupFs::new()); calls fs.probe().await; asserts Ok(()); asserts /sys/fs/cgroup/.overdrive-probe-* does NOT exist post-call (probe self-cleans); pre-test snapshot of /sys/fs/cgroup contents equals post-test snapshot (proves no leaked state)

C-probe-with-custom-root Tier 3 test exists; uses tempfile::TempDir; constructs RealCgroupFs::new().with_probe_root(tmp.path().to_path_buf()); asserts probe returns Ok(()); asserts post-probe tmp.path() is empty (probe directory was removed); asserts /sys/fs/cgroup contents byte-identical pre/post (proves the override scopes — probe did NOT touch /sys/fs/cgroup at all)

crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs gains `mod real_cgroup_fs;` block (or extends an existing block per the inline-mod-wrapper trick per .claude/rules/testing.md § Layout — integration tests live under tests/integration/)
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration) and test(real_cgroup_fs)' runs the two C-probe-* scenarios and they pass

Per-step mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-host --file crates/overdrive-host/src/cgroup_fs.rs achieves kill rate ≥ 80%

steps.name: Add SimCgroupFs adapter in overdrive-sim with injection schedule + BTreeMap byte store
steps.description: Lands the test binding of CgroupFs per ADR-0054 § Sim adapter. In-memory BTreeMap<PathBuf, (SimEntry, Vec<u8>)> byte store guarded by parking_lot::Mutex (no .await while holding); per-(SimOp, PathBuf) error injection schedule keyed by BTreeMap<(u8, PathBuf), VecDeque<io::ErrorKind>> for deterministic iteration per .claude/rules/development.md § Ordered-collection choice. Exposes inject_error(SimOp, PathBuf, ErrorKind) for test fault scenarios; exposes snapshot() -> BTreeMap<PathBuf, (SimEntry, Vec<u8>)> as test-only inspection. Method-entry deterministic per ADR-0054 § D4 (every mutation happens before the first .await; cancellation = no-mutation-yet or fully-completed; never partial). Re-exports SimCgroupFs from overdrive-sim::adapters::mod and the crate root. Adds overdrive-sim.workspace = true under overdrive-worker [dev-dependencies] per the DISTILL § Pre-requisites note. Lands the bulk of Class B scenarios (18 scenarios + F1 K3 determinism guard) at the trait surface — every documented edge case from ADR-0054 § D1 method docstrings gets a SimCgroupFs-driven assertion.
steps.criteria: SimCgroupFs implements CgroupFs against an in-memory BTreeMap byte store with a BTreeMap-keyed injection schedule; every method body is method-entry deterministic (no .await between Mutex acquisition and mutation); kind() returns "overdrive_sim::SimCgroupFs"; snapshot() and inject_error() test helpers exist with documented call shapes; all 18 Class B scenarios + F1 + the 2 B-write-respects-* manager-equivalent scenarios run green at Tier 1 (default lane, no --features integration-tests).
steps.scenario_name: All Class B scenarios (B-create_dir-happy, B-create_dir-injected-permission-denied, B-write-happy, B-write-empty-bytes, B-write-missing-parent-returns-notfound, B-write-injected-other-returns, B-remove_dir-happy, B-remove_dir-notfound, B-remove_dir-directory-not-empty, B-remove_dir-injected-permission-denied, B-probe-happy, B-probe-injected-substrate-error, B-probe-round-trip-mismatch, B-error-schedule-determinism, B-write-then-snapshot-is-deterministic-across-runs, B-write-respects-cgroup-procs-pid-payload, B-write-cgroup-kill-stores-one-newline) + F1 (K3 determinism guard proptest)
acceptance_criteria: crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/cgroup_fs.rs exists implementing CgroupFs per ADR-0054 § Sim adapter; `pub struct SimCgroupFs { state: Arc<Mutex<BTreeMap<PathBuf, (SimEntry, Vec<u8>)>>>, errors: Arc<Mutex<BTreeMap<(u8, PathBuf), VecDeque<io::ErrorKind>>>> }` — both inner maps are BTreeMap (NOT HashMap) per .claude/rules/development.md § Ordered-collection choice
`pub enum SimEntry { Dir, File }` and `pub enum SimOp { CreateDir, Write, RemoveDir, Probe }` declared in the same file with Debug + Clone + Copy + PartialEq + Eq derives
SimCgroupFs::new() -> Self constructs empty state and empty injection schedule

SimCgroupFs::inject_error(&self, op: SimOp, path: PathBuf, kind: io::ErrorKind) pushes onto the per-(op, path) VecDeque — each matching call pops one entry; empty queue means the call proceeds normally (matches B-create_dir-injected-permission-denied behavior)
SimCgroupFs::snapshot(&self) -> BTreeMap<PathBuf, (SimEntry, Vec<u8>)> clones the entire state map for test inspection; doc comment marks this as TEST-HOOK-ONLY

#[async_trait] impl CgroupFs for SimCgroupFs has create_dir doing mkdir-p semantics (walking path.components(), inserting each ancestor as SimEntry::Dir with empty bytes if absent — matches B-create_dir-happy postcondition); write checking parent existence and returning NotFound if absent (matches B-write-missing-parent-returns-notfound); remove_dir returning NotFound if path absent AND DirectoryNotEmpty if path is a dir with children (matches B-remove_dir-notfound + B-remove_dir-directory-not-empty); probe() structurally round-tripping b"probe\n" through /sim-probe-root and asserting byte-equality, returning ProbeError::Substrate on injected io::Error, ProbeError::RoundTripMismatch on corrupted bytes (matches B-probe-injected-substrate-error + B-probe-round-trip-mismatch); kind() returning literal "overdrive_sim::SimCgroupFs" (matches B-kind-returns-stable-string)

Every method body acquires the parking_lot::Mutex, mutates BTreeMap, releases — NO .await while holding the guard (per .claude/rules/development.md § Concurrency & async — Never hold a lock across .await); the async fn surface exists only to satisfy the trait signature

Pre-mutation injection check: every method pops from the injection schedule BEFORE mutating state; on Err return, state is unchanged (matches B-write-injected-other-returns: snapshot does NOT contain the path)

SimCgroupFs and SimEntry and SimOp re-exported from crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/mod.rs (matches existing SimClock / SimEntropy / SimDriver re-export pattern) and from crates/overdrive-sim/src/lib.rs
crates/overdrive-worker/Cargo.toml gains `overdrive-sim.workspace = true` under [dev-dependencies]

All 18 Class B test files exist under crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/sim_cgroup_fs/ — one file per scenario family (create_dir.rs, write.rs, remove_dir.rs, probe.rs, kind.rs, error_schedule.rs); each scenario file constructs `let fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs> = Arc::new(SimCgroupFs::new());` and exercises the trait through Arc<dyn CgroupFs> (no concrete-type access — proves the trait IS the contract); snapshot inspection uses SimCgroupFs::snapshot() via downcast or via a wrapper-type test-only helper

F1 K3 determinism guard exists at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/sim_cgroup_fs/k3_determinism.rs — proptest at default case count 1024 generating Vec<Op> of length 100; asserts two fresh SimCgroupFs instances given the same op sequence produce bit-identical Result trajectory AND bit-identical final snapshots
crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance.rs gains `mod sim_cgroup_fs;` block per the inline-mod-wrapper convention
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker -E 'binary(acceptance) and test(sim_cgroup_fs)' runs all B + F1 scenarios green at Tier 1 (default lane, no --features integration-tests)

Per-step mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/cgroup_fs.rs achieves kill rate ≥ 80%

steps.name: Refactor cgroup_manager.rs: free functions become methods on CgroupManager struct holding Arc<dyn CgroupFs>
steps.description: Single-cut refactor of crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs per ADR-0054 § D5. Eight free functions (create_workload_scope, place_pid_in_scope, write_resource_limits, write_resource_limits_warn_on_error, cgroup_kill, remove_workload_scope, create_workloads_slice_with_controllers) collapse into method bodies on `pub struct CgroupManager { fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs>, cgroup_root: PathBuf }`. Mandatory-not-defaulted constructor: `pub fn new(cgroup_root: PathBuf, fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs>) -> Self`. Free helpers that don't touch FS stay free (cpu_weight_for, WorkloadsBootstrapError, CgroupPath). The async signatures convert tokio::fs::* calls to self.fs.create_dir(...).await / self.fs.write(...).await / self.fs.remove_dir(...).await. Processes E1 triage matrix rows 1-14: rows 1-5 stay inline (pure logic — cgroup_path_as_str_returns_canonical_string, cpu_weight_for_pins_division_and_clamp, three from_subtree_control_io_* tests); rows 6, 7, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14 convert to SimCgroupFs-backed CgroupManager tests under crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/*.rs. Rows 8 and 10 stay KEEP-TEMPFILE in place (defer move to step 01-05); rows 15 and 16 (KEEP-AND-MOVE bootstrap pair) move to step 01-07. The single-cut posture per feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations: no compatibility shims, no #[deprecated] on the old free-fn signatures — they are deleted.
steps.criteria: CgroupManager struct exists with mandatory-not-defaulted constructor; eight async method bodies exercise self.fs (NOT tokio::fs::* directly); pure-logic helpers (cpu_weight_for) stay as free functions; the 7 CONVERT rows from E1 land as SimCgroupFs-backed tests in tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/; the 5 KEEP-INLINE rows from E1 stay in #[cfg(test)] mod tests; old free-fn signatures are deleted (single-cut); cargo xtask dst-lint passes (no std::fs::* in async bodies); per-step mutation gate ≥ 80%.
steps.scenario_name: E1 rows 1-14: 5 KEEP-INLINE (rows 1-5: pure logic) + 7 CONVERT (rows 6, 7, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14: SimCgroupFs-backed CgroupManager tests). Rows 8+10 deferred to step 01-05; rows 15+16 deferred to step 01-07.
acceptance_criteria: crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs declares `pub struct CgroupManager { fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs>, cgroup_root: PathBuf }` matching ADR-0054 § D5 verbatim

`pub fn new(cgroup_root: PathBuf, fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs>) -> Self` — both parameters mandatory; NO `Default` impl; NO `with_fs` / `with_cgroup_root` builder; positional and required per .claude/rules/development.md § Port-trait dependencies

Method bodies on CgroupManager: async fn create_workload_scope, async fn place_pid_in_scope, async fn write_resource_limits, async fn write_resource_limits_warn_on_error, async fn cgroup_kill, async fn remove_workload_scope, fn create_workloads_slice_with_controllers (sync today; async per the keep-and-move triage in step 01-07). Bodies call self.fs.create_dir(...).await / self.fs.write(...).await / self.fs.remove_dir(...).await — NO direct tokio::fs::* references

Free helpers preserved: pub fn cpu_weight_for(cpu_milli: u32) -> u32 stays free (pure arithmetic, no FS); WorkloadsBootstrapError enum + from_subtree_control_io classifier stay as-is; CgroupPath newtype stays as-is

Old free-fn signatures DELETED per single-cut posture per feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations — no `#[deprecated]` markers, no compatibility shims, no re-export of the old shape; rg "^pub async fn (create_workload_scope|place_pid_in_scope|write_resource_limits|cgroup_kill|remove_workload_scope)" crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs returns ZERO matches at the module level (the names are now method names on CgroupManager, not free items)

E1 KEEP-INLINE rows 1-5 stay in crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tests: cgroup_path_as_str_returns_canonical_string, cpu_weight_for_pins_division_and_clamp, from_subtree_control_io_discriminates_ebusy_via_resource_busy_kind, from_subtree_control_io_discriminates_ebusy_via_raw_os_error, from_subtree_control_io_routes_non_ebusy_to_write_failed — verified by name

E1 CONVERT row 6 (cgroup_kill_is_idempotent_on_missing_scope) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/cgroup_kill_idempotent.rs — SimCgroupFs-backed, asserts manager.cgroup_kill(&scope).await returns Ok when scope is absent (verifies the manager's NotFound→Ok swallow logic layered on top of SimCgroupFs's B-remove_dir-notfound base behavior)

E1 CONVERT row 7 (cgroup_kill_writes_one_to_cgroup_kill_file) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/cgroup_kill_writes_one_newline.rs — constructs Arc::new(SimCgroupFs::new()), constructs CgroupManager, calls manager.cgroup_kill(&scope).await, asserts SimCgroupFs::snapshot() contains <scope>/cgroup.kill with bytes b"1\n"

E1 CONVERT row 9 (remove_workload_scope_is_idempotent_on_missing_scope) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/remove_workload_scope_idempotent.rs — SimCgroupFs returns NotFound (per B-remove_dir-notfound); wrapper swallows to Ok

E1 CONVERT row 11 (create_workload_scope_writes_a_real_directory) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/create_workload_scope.rs — asserts snapshot() contains <scope> typed SimEntry::Dir

E1 CONVERT row 12 (place_pid_in_scope_writes_pid_to_cgroup_procs) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/place_pid_in_scope.rs — manager.place_pid_in_scope(&scope, 1234).await; asserts snapshot() at <scope>/cgroup.procs is (SimEntry::File, b"1234\n".to_vec())

E1 CONVERT row 13 (write_resource_limits_writes_cpu_weight_and_memory_max) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/write_resource_limits.rs — asserts snapshot() reflects both <scope>/cpu.weight and <scope>/memory.max byte payloads

E1 CONVERT row 14 (write_resource_limits_warn_on_error_writes_files_on_success) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/write_resource_limits_warn_on_error.rs — asserts snapshot() reflects both files on the success path (the warn-and-continue path is exercised separately via inject_error)
crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance.rs gains `mod cgroup_manager;` block per the inline-mod-wrapper convention
cargo xtask dst-lint passes — verifies no std::fs::* inside async fn bodies in the refactored cgroup_manager.rs (per .claude/rules/development.md § No blocking std::fs::* inside async fn)
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker -E 'binary(acceptance) and test(cgroup_manager)' runs all 7 CONVERT scenarios green at Tier 1

cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --all-features completes with no errors (the old free-fn deletions must not leave dangling references; if step 01-05 hasn't landed yet, ExecDriver's internal call sites continue to compile against the new CgroupManager surface either via a transitional shim INSIDE the worker crate's private module OR by co-landing the relevant ExecDriver edits in this step — crafter's choice; the constraint is a clean cargo check at every commit boundary)

Per-step mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs achieves kill rate ≥ 80%

steps.name: ExecDriver::new gains mandatory fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs> parameter; migrate all 11 existing exec_driver tests in lockstep
steps.description: Single-cut signature change on ExecDriver per ADR-0054 § D5 step 5. `pub fn new(cgroup_root: PathBuf, clock: Arc<dyn Clock>, fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs>) -> Self` — third parameter mandatory; NO Default impl; NO builder. Internally constructs `CgroupManager::new(cgroup_root.clone(), fs.clone())` and replaces every direct cgroup_manager::xxx call with self.cgroup_manager.xxx. Every existing fixture in crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/*.rs (11 files per the C-existing-integration-suite-passes scenario count) migrates in the same commit, threading Arc::new(RealCgroupFs::new()) as the third constructor argument — pure mechanical migration, NO assertion changes. The A1 + A2 trybuild fixtures go GREEN at this step (A1 = missing-arg arity; A2 = no-Default). Processes E1 KEEP-TEMPFILE rows 8 and 10 (cgroup_kill_propagates_non_notfound_errors and remove_workload_scope_propagates_non_notfound_errors): MOVES them out of src/cgroup_manager.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tests INTO crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/cgroup_manager/*.rs gated behind --features integration-tests (per E1 matrix rationale — they stay tempfile-backed against RealCgroupFs because they assert on real-substrate ENOTDIR which SimCgroupFs cannot model). steps.name: Binary composition root: instantiate RealCgroupFs, probe(), thread Arc<dyn CgroupFs> through worker subsystem to ExecDriver::new
steps.description: Wires the production binding at the binary boundary per ADR-0054 § Composition root wiring. The overdrive-cli `serve` subcommand (or wherever the worker subsystem is instantiated) constructs `let fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs> = Arc::new(overdrive_host::RealCgroupFs::new());`, calls `fs.probe().await` BEFORE worker subsystem startup, and threads fs.clone() into the worker entrypoint which threads it into ExecDriver::new. On probe failure, emits a structured `health.startup.refused` event whose body carries the ProbeError cause and exits non-zero per ADR-0054 § Composition root wiring. The probe runs BEFORE the existing cgroup_preflight check from ADR-0028 (preserving the staged refusal order: probe = adapter-can-round-trip-bytes; preflight = cgroup-v2-delegation-exists). AppState gains NO field — the Arc<dyn CgroupFs> flows through the worker subsystem entrypoint, NOT through AppState (preserves the ADR-0029 invariant that overdrive-control-plane does NOT depend on overdrive-worker and does NOT name CgroupFs). Lands the E2 walking-skeleton scenario: subprocess invocation of `overdrive serve` with a probe configured to fail (via test-only CLI flag OR env var the binary honors — crafter picks) produces the health.startup.refused event and exits non-zero within ≤ 5s; convergence loop never starts.
steps.criteria: overdrive-cli serve subcommand instantiates RealCgroupFs once at boot, probes it before worker startup, threads the Arc<dyn CgroupFs> through to ExecDriver::new; probe failure emits health.startup.refused with ProbeError cause and exits non-zero; AppState has no cgroup_fs field (preserves ADR-0029 boundary); E2 walking-skeleton scenario passes; the existing 11 exec_driver integration tests AND any existing overdrive-cli serve tests still pass.
steps.scenario_name: E2 (composition-root probe gate walking-skeleton: subprocess startup-refusal with health.startup.refused event when probe fails)
acceptance_criteria: crates/overdrive-cli/src/<serve-subcommand-file>.rs (likely serve.rs or main.rs's serve handler) constructs `let fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs> = Arc::new(overdrive_host::RealCgroupFs::new());` exactly once at boot, BEFORE the worker subsystem is started

`fs.probe().await` is called BEFORE any cgroup write the worker would otherwise issue AND BEFORE the existing cgroup_preflight check from ADR-0028; the probe is the FIRST adapter-level health check after the binary parses args and resolves config

On Err(ProbeError) from probe, the binary emits a structured `health.startup.refused` event (via the existing tracing/structured-log surface — crafter consults the existing health.* event taxonomy in the codebase) whose body carries: (a) the event name health.startup.refused, (b) the ProbeError Display string AS A FIELD (typed; NOT collapsed to Internal(String) per .claude/rules/development.md § Never flatten a typed error to Internal(String) at a composition boundary), (c) sufficient cause to point an operator at the substrate problem (overlayfs no-op, permission denied, etc.)
On probe failure the binary process exits non-zero (likely exit code 1 or a dedicated reservation per existing CLI conventions) — verified by E2 subprocess assertion

The Arc<dyn CgroupFs> is threaded into the worker subsystem entrypoint (likely Worker::start(config, obs, fs.clone()).await? per ADR-0054 § Composition root wiring pseudocode); the worker subsystem entrypoint internally passes fs.clone() into ExecDriver::new

AppState does NOT gain a cgroup_fs field; crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs has no `pub struct AppState { ..., cgroup_fs: Arc<dyn CgroupFs>, ... }` regression; overdrive-control-plane Cargo.toml does NOT gain `overdrive-host = ...` or `overdrive-core` impl-of-trait usage of CgroupFs — the trait name CgroupFs MUST NOT appear in overdrive-control-plane source per ADR-0054 § Composition root wiring (preserves ADR-0029 invariant)
rg 'CgroupFs' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src returns ZERO matches (structural defense: the control-plane crate never names the trait)

Probe runs BEFORE cgroup_preflight (the existing ADR-0028 gate). Staged order at boot: (1) parse args + load config; (2) fs.probe(); (3) cgroup_preflight; (4) worker subsystem start; (5) AppState construction; (6) HTTP/gRPC bind. Verified by code-path inspection AND by E2 asserting probe failure exits BEFORE cgroup_preflight runs (E2 asserts cgroup_preflight is never invoked when probe fails)

E2 walking-skeleton scenario lives at crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/serve_probe_refusal.rs (preferred — subprocess form per .claude/rules/testing.md driving-adapter convention) OR at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/composition_root_probe_gate.rs (acceptable — in-process Worker::start form); crafter picks the cleanest exposure. The test mechanism for forcing probe failure is either a test-only CLI flag (--probe-root <unwritable-tmpdir>) OR an env var the binary honors (e.g. OVERDRIVE_PROBE_ROOT=<unwritable-path>); crafter picks the mechanism that doesn't pollute the production CLI surface

E2 assertions: (a) subprocess exits non-zero within ≤ 5 seconds; (b) stderr or structured-log capture contains a health.startup.refused event with ProbeError::Substrate { source } cause; (c) source.kind() is one of PermissionDenied / ReadOnlyFilesystem / similar (matching the test's chosen unwritable mechanism); (d) the worker convergence loop never started (no allocations accepted; the HTTP/gRPC port is never bound — verified by attempting a TCP connect to the expected port and getting ECONNREFUSED)

cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests runs E2 green AND all 11 existing exec_driver tests still green AND any existing overdrive-cli serve integration tests still green
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --all-features completes with no errors

steps.name: Bootstrap pair keep-and-move (E1 rows 15+16); equivalence proptest (D1); K3 determinism guard (F1 wired)
steps.description: Processes the remaining E1 matrix entries that need structural movement. Both tests move to crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/workloads_slice_bootstrap.rs (SimCgroupFs-backed per the DISTILL E1 matrix decision — the bootstrap function's async conversion enables the SimCgroupFs surface). Lands D1: the Real/Sim equivalence proptest (reviewer carry-over #1) at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/cgroup_fs_equivalence.rs gated behind --features integration-tests — proptest sequences of {CreateDir, Write, RemoveDir} ops against both RealCgroupFs-rooted-at-tempdir AND SimCgroupFs, asserting same-Ok/same-Err verdict per op + byte-equal snapshot after the sequence; default 1024 cases. Confirms F1 (K3 determinism guard) from step 01-03 is wired into the default test lane (the proptest harness already lands at 01-03; this step verifies it runs as part of the standard CI surface and the assertion is genuinely structural).
steps.criteria: create_workloads_slice_with_controllers is async fn (method on CgroupManager); E1 rows 15+16 land as SimCgroupFs-backed bootstrap tests at tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/workloads_slice_bootstrap.rs; D1 equivalence proptest exists and passes at default case count 1024; F1 K3 determinism guard is part of the default test lane and asserts bit-identical trajectories.
steps.scenario_name: E1 rows 15+16 (KEEP-AND-MOVE bootstrap pair → SimCgroupFs-backed); D1 (Real/Sim equivalence proptest); F1 (K3 determinism guard — wired into the default lane)
acceptance_criteria: crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs: pub async fn create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(&self) -> Result<(), WorkloadsBootstrapError> — method on CgroupManager, async, uses self.fs.create_dir(...).await + self.fs.write(...).await for the slice + subtree_control writes; ALL prior std::fs::* references inside this function are removed; cargo xtask dst-lint passes
Callers of create_workloads_slice_with_controllers (likely in the worker subsystem startup path) update to the new async fn signature in the same step's commits

E1 row 15 test (create_workloads_slice_with_controllers_creates_dir_and_writes_subtree_control) lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/acceptance/cgroup_manager/workloads_slice_bootstrap.rs as a SimCgroupFs-backed #[tokio::test]: constructs Arc::new(SimCgroupFs::new()), constructs CgroupManager, awaits manager.create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(), asserts snapshot() contains <root>/overdrive.slice/workloads.slice as SimEntry::Dir AND snapshot() contains <root>/overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/cgroup.subtree_control as SimEntry::File with the expected controller payload (e.g. b"+cpu +memory ...\n")

E1 row 16 test (create_workloads_slice_with_controllers_is_idempotent) lands in the same file (or sibling) as a SimCgroupFs-backed #[tokio::test] invoking the method twice sequentially against the same SimCgroupFs instance and asserting (a) both invocations return Ok, (b) snapshot() is byte-identical after invocation 1 and invocation 2 (idempotency proven via snapshot comparison)
rg "create_workloads_slice_with_controllers_(creates_dir|is_idempotent)" crates/overdrive-worker/src returns ZERO matches (both moved out of inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests)

D1 equivalence proptest exists at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/cgroup_fs_equivalence.rs gated behind --features integration-tests (Tier 3 — touches real fs via tempfile per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating): generates Vec<Op> where Op = CreateDir(PathBuf) | Write(PathBuf, Vec<u8>) | RemoveDir(PathBuf) with PathBuf restricted to a small bounded alphabet (e.g. /{a,b,c}/{0,1,2}) and Vec<u8> bounded to ≤ 16 bytes; constructs `let real_root = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("tempdir")`; `let real_fs = Arc::new(RealCgroupFs::new())`; `let sim_fs = Arc::new(SimCgroupFs::new())`; applies the sequence to both adapters (Real paths prefixed with real_root.path(), Sim paths prefixed with /); asserts per-op Ok/Err verdict matches AND post-sequence snapshot equivalence (Real via tokio::fs::read at touched paths; Sim via snapshot().get(P))

D1 docstring explicitly notes the LIMITATION per the DISTILL scenario: validates BYTE-STORE contract only; kernel-side effects (cgroup.kill mass-kill, subtree_control EBUSY, pseudo-file synthesis, EINVAL) are EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE per ADR-0054 § D3 and live in Class C scenarios (step 01-08). RealCgroupFs operates against the tempdir root — NOT against /sys/fs/cgroup — because this property tests the BYTE-STORE contract, not kernel semantics
D1 runs at proptest default case count 1024 per .claude/rules/testing.md § Property-based testing → CI runs default case count per PR
D1 wired via crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs `mod cgroup_fs_equivalence;` block

F1 K3 determinism guard (landed at step 01-03) runs as part of `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker -E 'binary(acceptance) and test(k3_determinism)'` and passes at default case count 1024 — verified by re-running and confirming green
cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests runs E1-15/16 + D1 + F1 all green AND no regressions in any prior step's tests

Per-step mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs achieves kill rate ≥ 80% (mutations to create_workloads_slice_with_controllers's new async body must be killed by the moved tests)

steps.name: Tier 3 kernel-semantics coverage: Class C scenarios for the new CgroupFs port (cgroup.kill, subtree_control EBUSY, controller validation, pseudo-file synthesis, rmdir auto-reap, PID movement, read-only pseudo-file EACCES)
steps.description: Final Tier 3 audit per ADR-0054 § D3 + DISTILL Class C scenarios. Lands 7 kernel-side-effect scenarios that prove SimCgroupFs's non-replacement contract is honest (Sim cannot model these; only RealCgroupFs against real /sys/fs/cgroup via Lima sudo can). All scenarios use the per-test AllocCleanup RAII guard pattern from crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/cleanup.rs (per .claude/rules/testing.md § Leaked workload cgroups). Scenarios: C-cgroup-kill (write b"1\n" to cgroup.kill terminates every PID in scope within ≤ 2s); C-subtree-control-ebusy (writing +memory with live child returns EBUSY; WorkloadsBootstrapError::from_subtree_control_io dispatches to SubtreeControlBusy variant); C-controller-validation (out-of-range cpu.weight returns EINVAL); C-pseudo-file-synthesis (cgroup.events appears at mkdir-time matching `(?m)^(populated|frozen) [01]$`); C-rmdir-auto-reap (empty scope rmdir succeeds; kernel reaps pseudo-files); C-procs-pid-movement (writing PID to cgroup.procs actually moves the process; /proc/<pid>/cgroup reflects new scope); C-write-to-readonly-cgroup-file (writing to cgroup.events (kernel-read-only pseudo-file) via CgroupManager propagates real EACCES through the manager — the new production-realistic substrate-boundary scenario replacing the ENOTDIR-via-regular-file contrivance). Confirms C-existing-integration-suite-passes still green (regression assertion). Confirms zero leftover cgroups across the suite per the detection one-liner in .claude/rules/testing.md.
steps.criteria: All 7 Class C scenarios exist as Tier 3 integration tests gated behind --features integration-tests; each runs green via cargo xtask lima run --; each uses AllocCleanup RAII to guarantee scope reaping on panic; C-existing-integration-suite-passes regression-asserts the 11 exec_driver tests still green; leftover-cgroup detection one-liner returns empty after the full suite; the entire feature is structurally complete with ≥ 80% mutation kill rate on the per-PR `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests` (no --file scope) covering the full diff against origin/main.
steps.scenario_name: C-cgroup-kill, C-subtree-control-ebusy, C-controller-validation, C-pseudo-file-synthesis, C-rmdir-auto-reap, C-procs-pid-movement, C-write-to-readonly-cgroup-file (all 7 Tier 3 kernel-semantics scenarios); C-existing-integration-suite-passes (regression re-check)
acceptance_criteria: C-cgroup-kill lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/real_cgroup_fs/cgroup_kill_terminates_pids.rs gated behind --features integration-tests: constructs `let fs = Arc::new(RealCgroupFs::new())`; creates workload scope `overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/alloc-killC-0.scope` under /sys/fs/cgroup; spawns `/bin/sleep 3600` into the scope (via nix::unistd::fork + execve + write-PID-to-cgroup.procs per the existing exec_driver integration test pattern); calls fs.write(<scope>/cgroup.kill, b"1\n").await; asserts Ok(()); polls waitpid(WNOHANG) up to 2 seconds wall-clock; asserts the sleep PID has been reaped; AllocCleanup Drop reaps the empty scope

C-subtree-control-ebusy lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/real_cgroup_fs/subtree_control_ebusy.rs: creates overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/alloc-EBUSY-0.scope containing a live /bin/sleep 3600; calls fs.write(overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/cgroup.subtree_control, b"+memory\n").await; asserts Err(e) where e.kind() == ErrorKind::ResourceBusy OR e.raw_os_error() == Some(libc::EBUSY); calls WorkloadsBootstrapError::from_subtree_control_io(e) and asserts the result is SubtreeControlBusy { source } (preserves the typed EBUSY discrimination from before the refactor)

C-controller-validation lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/real_cgroup_fs/controller_validation.rs: creates a workload scope with cpu.weight writable; calls fs.write(<scope>/cpu.weight, b"99999999\n").await (cgroup v2 accepts 1..=10000); asserts Err(e) where e.kind() == ErrorKind::InvalidInput OR e.raw_os_error() == Some(libc::EINVAL)

C-pseudo-file-synthesis lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/real_cgroup_fs/pseudo_file_synthesis.rs: creates a workload scope; reads <scope>/cgroup.events via tokio::fs::read (NOT via CgroupFs::write — the trait has no read method; the scenario asserts on substrate behavior the probe relies on); asserts the file exists and body matches regex `(?m)^(populated|frozen) [01]$`

C-rmdir-auto-reap lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/real_cgroup_fs/rmdir_auto_reap.rs: creates a workload scope with NO live PIDs in cgroup.procs; calls fs.remove_dir(<scope>).await; asserts Ok(()); asserts <scope> no longer exists; asserts the test did NOT need to individually remove pseudo-files (cgroup.procs, cpu.weight, memory.max — they were reaped automatically by rmdir(2))

C-procs-pid-movement lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/real_cgroup_fs/procs_pid_movement.rs: creates a workload scope; spawns /bin/sleep 3600 in the test process's cgroup (NOT yet in ); calls fs.write(/cgroup.procs, format!("{P}\n").as_bytes()).await; asserts Ok(()); reads /proc/

/cgroup and asserts the line ends with (PID is now in ); AllocCleanup reaps via cgroup.kill + rmdir

C-write-to-readonly-cgroup-file lands at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/cgroup_manager/write_to_readonly_cgroup_file.rs gated behind --features integration-tests: constructs `let fs = Arc::new(RealCgroupFs::new())` AND a CgroupManager around it; creates workload scope alloc-roC-0.scope; invokes a CgroupManager operation that ends up calling fs.write(<scope>/cgroup.events, ...) (the test mechanism is either a test-only helper on CgroupManager that takes a relative pseudo-file name OR direct invocation of the same underlying fs.write path the public surface uses — crafter picks); asserts Err(e) where e.kind() == ErrorKind::PermissionDenied OR e.raw_os_error() == Some(libc::EACCES); asserts the error has propagated through CgroupManager from the REAL tokio::fs::write syscall (the substrate-boundary propagation chain real kernel VFS → tokio::fs::* → CgroupFs trait method → CgroupManager caller is intact); AllocCleanup reaps the empty scope on Drop
Every Class C test wires AllocCleanup RAII (or equivalent on-Drop reaping mechanism) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Leaked workload cgroups — Prevention
crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration.rs gains all new Class C scenario modules in the `mod real_cgroup_fs` and `mod cgroup_manager` inline blocks

cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests -E 'binary(integration)' runs ALL Class C scenarios green AND all 11 existing exec_driver tests still green (C-existing-integration-suite-passes regression PASS)

Leftover-cgroup detection one-liner from .claude/rules/testing.md § Leaked workload cgroups (the canonical `cargo xtask lima run -- bash -lc 'pgrep -af "/bin/sleep"...'` shape) returns empty after the full suite — zero leftover allocs
Per-step mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file <step's-touched-files> achieves kill rate ≥ 80%

Final per-PR mutation gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker (NO --file flag — full per-package diff) achieves kill rate ≥ 80% per AC8 (collective mutation gate ≥ 80% on overdrive-worker post-migration); also run for overdrive-core (CgroupFs trait + ProbeError) and overdrive-host (RealCgroupFs) and overdrive-sim (SimCgroupFs) packages — every package this PR touched must hit ≥ 80%
